_comment: Auto-publish queue. The GitHub Action publishes queue[0] three times a week (Mon/Wed/Fri 09:00 UTC), then removes it. Newest-intended-first is NOT required; order = publish order (top publishes first). To add a post: write _drafts/<slug>.body.txt, add assets/images/og-<slug>.svg + rendered .png, and prepend/append an entry here. Fields: slug, title, description, og_title, og_desc, og_alt, tag (visible), cat (filter key: partnerships|growth|leadgen|operations|automation), read, summary (listing card).

queue.title: Channel Partnerships: A Playbook for Partner-Led Growth
queue.description: Someone already sells to your exact buyer. Channel partnerships let you grow through their trust, but only when the partner's win is concrete and selling you is effortless.
queue.og_title: Channel Partnerships: A Playbook for Partner-Led Growth
queue.og_desc: Grow through other companies' trust, not just your own outreach — how to build a channel that actually sells instead of collecting logos.
queue.summary: Someone already sells to your exact buyer. Channel partnerships let you grow through their trust, but only when the partner's win is concrete and selling you is effortless.

queue.title: Integration Partnerships: The Channel That Sells Itself
queue.description: When your product connects to a partner's, the case for both of you sells itself in the product, not in a slide. How to build integrations that actually produce pipeline.
queue.og_title: Integration Partnerships: The Channel That Sells Itself
queue.og_desc: A working integration proves the overlap between two products instead of asserting it. Build it, list it, maintain it, and choose a few over many.
queue.summary: When your product connects to a partner's, the case for both of you sells itself in the product, not in a slide. How to build integrations that actually produce pipeline.

queue.title: Marketing Automation: Workflows That Don't Annoy Your Leads
queue.description: Automation earns a bad reputation when it runs on a calendar instead of a lead's actual behavior. How to build workflows that react to reality instead of a schedule.
queue.og_title: Marketing Automation: Workflows That Don't Annoy Your Leads
queue.og_desc: The gap between helpful automation and annoying automation is whether it reacts to what a lead does or just runs on a schedule.
queue.summary: Automation earns a bad reputation when it runs on a calendar instead of a lead's actual behavior. How to build workflows that react to reality instead of a schedule.

queue.title: The Sales-to-Success Handoff: Where Deals Go Cold
queue.description: A deal closes and the context that won it usually stays in the closing rep's head. What actually has to transfer to delivery, and how to build a handoff that carries it.
queue.og_title: The Sales-to-Success Handoff: Where Deals Go Cold
queue.og_desc: Deals rarely die at the handoff because delivery did a bad job. They die because the context that won the deal never made it to the team responsible for keeping it.
queue.summary: A deal closes and the context that won it usually stays in the closing rep's head. What actually has to transfer to delivery, and how to build a handoff that carries it.

queue.title: CRM Hygiene: The Automation Nobody Wants to Own
queue.description: A messy CRM taxes every system built on top of it, from forecasts to lead scoring to automation. Why prevention at data entry beats quarterly cleanup projects.
queue.og_title: CRM Hygiene: The Automation Nobody Wants to Own
queue.og_desc: Every automation and forecast built on top of dirty data inherits its mistakes at scale. How to prevent the mess instead of cleaning it up quarterly.
queue.summary: A messy CRM taxes every system built on top of it, from forecasts to lead scoring to automation. Why prevention at data entry beats quarterly cleanup projects.
